label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. So the problem is about Jane's birthday and finding a date 10 days before that. Let me start by breaking down the information given. First, Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. Wait, February's last day is usually the 28th, unless it's a leap year when it's the 29th. But 2001 isn't a leap year, right? Leap years are every 4 years, like 2000, 2004, etc. So 2001 isn't a leap year, so the last day of February would be February 28, 2001. So Jane's birthday is 02/28. Now, today is her 16-year-old birthday. That means today's date is her 16th birthday. So if she was born in 2001, adding 16 years would make today's date 2017. Specifically, her birthday in 2017 would be February 28, 2017. But wait, is 2017 a leap year? Let's check. 2016 was a leap year, then the next one is 2020. So 2017 isn't a leap year. Therefore, February 2017 has 28 days. So today is 02/28/2017. The question asks for the date 10 days ago from today. So if today is February 28, 2017, subtracting 10 days would take us back to... Let's calculate. Let me count backward. Starting from February 28, subtract 1 day: Feb 27. Then 2 days: Feb 26. 3 days: Feb 25. 4: 24, 5:23, 6:22, 7:21, 8:20, 9:19, 10 days ago: Feb 18. Wait, that would be February 18, 2017. But looking at the options, (B) is 02/18/2017. But let me double-check my math because sometimes months can be tricky. Wait, February has 28 days in 2017. So from Feb 28, subtract 10 days. So 28 minus 10 is 18. So yes, that would be February 18, 2017. Therefore, the answer should be (B). But wait, let me check the options again. The options are: (A) 02/28/2017 (B) 02/18/2017 (C) 03/18/2017 (D) 02/13/2017 (E) 02/25/2017
